金 Kintsugi
Docs GitHub v0.1.0
v0.1.0 · pre-alpha

Mend the wallet. Keep the gold.

Atomic, sweeper-proof wallet rescue using EIP-7702. Open source. Runs locally. No fees, ever.

$ npm install -g @kintsugi/cli
Read the docs
The problem

Your keys are leaked. Your wallet is being watched.

A sweeper bot now monitors the compromised address around the clock. The moment any ETH lands there to pay gas for a recovery transaction, the bot front-runs you and drains it in the same block. Tokens, NFTs, and ENS names sit visible but unreachable. Most rescues fail before they start.

Kintsugi removes the gas dependency entirely. The victim wallet never holds ETH at any point. A separate rescuer wallet pays for and submits the recovery in one atomic Type-4 transaction, transferring every selected asset out before the sweeper sees an opening.

Block N victim ← 0.01 ETH from your wallet +10⁻²
Block N sweeper → 0.01 ETH to attacker drained
Block N+1 your rescue tx → reverted no gas
Result tokens, NFTs, ENS still on the compromised wallet stranded
The recovery gap

They take what's liquid. They leave what's yours.

Drainer bots optimize for fast liquidation. They sweep ETH and the most liquid ERC-20s, ignore everything that takes effort to fence, and move on. The leftovers are often the most personally valuable holdings, but a working sweeper still blocks any normal recovery attempt.

字

ENS names

Your dot-eth identity, the one you've used everywhere for years, is rarely auto-drained. It's also typically the hardest to move once a sweeper is watching. Kintsugi handles unwrapped 2LDs, wrapped names, and subdomains in a single batch.

画

Illiquid NFTs

Long-tail collections, art, in-game items, memberships. Bots usually skip ERC-721 and ERC-1155 because the resale path is slow. They stay on the compromised wallet, visible to everyone, recoverable by no one.

小

Low-cap tokens

Reward tokens, governance positions, vested allocations, anything without a liquid pool. Drainers ignore them, but you still cannot reach them through a wallet the sweeper is gating.

How it works

One transaction. No gas from you. Nothing left behind.

EIP-7702 lets a victim wallet temporarily delegate execution to a battle-tested rescue contract. Combined with EIP-712 batch authorization, every selected asset moves to your safe wallet inside one atomic Type-4 transaction, paid for by a separate rescuer key.

1

You sign locally

The CLI signs an EIP-7702 authorization plus an EIP-712 batch of transfers (ERC-20, NFTs, ENS). Nothing leaves your machine yet. The victim private key is held in memory only and never written to disk.

2

Rescuer submits

A separate rescuer wallet you control submits a single Type-4 transaction. It pays the gas, attaches your authorization, and calls the audited rescue contract on your behalf. Your wallet's ETH balance never rises above zero.

3

Assets land safely

Inside the same transaction, the contract executes every transfer to a fresh safe address you control. The sweeper sees the rescue land, finds nothing to grab, and moves on. The victim wallet is then optionally revoked back to a pure EOA.

The victim wallet's balance never rises above zero. There is no opening for a sweeper to act on, because there is nothing to take.
Two ways in

A guided UI for panic. A pure CLI for power.

Same atomic flow under the hood, two surfaces to drive it. The web UI is launched from the CLI as a localhost server. The browser only sees session-bound addresses and signatures. Private keys never leave the local Node process.

The web UI

Start with kintsugi ui and a localhost browser tab walks you through five steps: network, wallets, discovery, plan, submit. Friendly for someone in panic mode.

Walk through the UI 
# launch the guided localhost UI
$ kintsugi ui

# ...or do everything in the terminal
$ kintsugi rescue

# inspect a wallet read-only first
$ kintsugi status 0xVictim...

# after rescue, optionally clear delegation
$ kintsugi revoke

The CLI

The same end-to-end rescue, fully scriptable. Inspect wallets, run interactive rescues, revoke delegation. Pipes well, no browser required.

Read the CLI reference
Or just ask your agent

In panic? Don't read. Ask.

Kintsugi ships a free agent skill following the agentskills.io spec. Install it once and Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Cline, Gemini CLI, or any compatible agent can drive the entire flow for you in plain English. The ordering rules and gotchas are baked in so you don't have to memorize them.

$ npx skills add ophelios-studio/skills --skill kintsugi
See what to ask
Our stance

Open source. No fees. No upsell.

A wallet rescue tool that charges money in the worst moment of someone's crypto life is a wallet rescue tool taking advantage of panic. We will not do that.

Kintsugi is MIT-licensed. The contracts are verified. The library, CLI, and UI are all in one public repository. There is no SaaS tier, no telemetry, no analytics, no signup. Run it yourself, audit every line, pay nothing but your own gas.

Repo github.com/ophelios-studio/kintsugi Sepolia rescue 0x53c1f40c…21fd5 Sepolia tracker 0x717883ab…47963 Mainnet pending audit
Three ways forward

Where do you want to start?

Right now

I'm in trouble. Walk me through a rescue.

Five minutes from install to your assets safely sitting in a fresh wallet. Plain language, no Solidity required.

Start the 5 minute rescue → First

I want to understand it before I run it.

The sweeper problem, EIP-7702 in plain English, the rescuer wallet pattern, why the rescue is atomic.

Read the concepts → Auditor mode

Show me the trust assumptions and contracts.

Threat model, what the rescuer can and cannot do, known limits, how to report a vulnerability, links to verified contracts.

Read the security docs →
